signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.41; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% — 375bp carry differential favors USD. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD LF net long +41,675 (CFTC, week ending Apr 14), long%=72.0% — extreme long positioning. HV30 at 6.8%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.4 vol-pt premium over realized. AUD/USD at 0.7162; 25d RR at −0.21: mild put skew. Late-trend caution signal — watch for LF delta turning negative to invalidate.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp GBP carry neutral. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. ATM IV 9.5% near HV, 25d RR flat — options neutral. GBP/USD at 1.351; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Positioning + Retail Sentiment
signals.text: NZD net short positioning at -17,090 (21.7% long) — not an extreme but aligned with price action. Retail sentiment shows an extreme short bias at 33% long / 67% short. The combination of net short positioning and retail sentiment extreme suggests potential for a short squeeze. Watch for a move above 0.5930 to confirm.
